text.title: Batching open questions across tickets
text.summary: Open questions surfaced by per-ticket analyzers are aggregated, deduplicated, and presented to the user as one coherent batch instead of N fragmented prompts.

rule.statement: The clarification collector MUST aggregate questions across all analyzers before any `AskUserQuestion` call.
rule.rationale: Asking the user ten times in a row destroys flow and produces inconsistent answers (the user forgets earlier choices). One coherent batch lets the user see all trade-offs at once and answer in a connected way.

list.item: Deduplicate — if N tickets ask the same scope question, merge into one.
Group by theme: scope, environment, data-vs-code path, hand-off to another team.
Order by criticality — blocker questions first; without an answer the orchestrator cannot proceed.
Cap each `AskUserQuestion` call to four questions to leave headroom under the system limit; split into multiple sequential calls if needed.
Each option must be a concrete labelled choice with a short description, not free-text.

antipattern.description: Asking the user a question whose answer can be derived by reading the codebase or running a small probe.
antipattern.reason: It signals the orchestrator did not investigate. Investigate first; ask only when investigation cannot resolve the choice.

antipattern.description: Asking trivial yes/no questions individually instead of merging similar ones.
antipattern.reason: The user reads each prompt linearly; many trivial prompts dilute attention from the actually-hard decisions.

section.title: Persistence
section.p: Answers are written to a per-ticket `clarifications.md` inside that ticket's feature folder. The planner subagent reads this file in its workflow so the human's decisions become part of the SDD record and survive across runs.
